programmes.show_description: This Is CBeebies. Programmes start at 6.00am.
Snakes and Ladders. Stuck indoors for the day, Tish and Bobby play the board game Snakes and Ladders.
Tree. Children's animation. Bing wants to dig the biggest hole and gets upset when Pando accidentally fills it in. It takes Flop to show them how they can work together.
Bouncing. Preschool series. Laa-Laa and Po drive the Tubby Car to the Hidey Hup and take turns on the Tubby Bouncy. Tinky Winky and Dipsy arrive and they have a go at bouncing too.
Den. Preschool animation. Tee and Lily get creative building their own dens, but it all gets rather competitive. Mo gets creative too in an effort to bridge the divide!
Double Doughnut Rescue. Supertato almost quits being a superhero when he can't save two friends at the same time.
Vaccination. Bing is brave for his vaccination as he is looking forward to the Hoppity Voosh sticker Dr Molly will give him afterwards. But then he finds out that there are none left!
Nine-Legged Bill. Cap'n Gail's tall tale of a nine-legged octopus in Lake Swoppiton has everyone panicking. But Mojo and Bo wonder: is the story real, or is Cap'n Gail up to something?
Hey Quacky! Worm and Ladybird wonder what's going on at Squirrel Club. There's a gorilla playing drums, some helpful ducks, a hot frog, four goats and a hospital run by chicken doctors.
The Breakfast Badge. Tag is feeling grumpy because he didn't eat his breakfast. Luckily, Duggee has his Breakfast Badge, and soon the Squirrels will too.
Pavlova. Mum won't let Bingo have pavlova, so the kids find a way by playing a cheeky game of cafe.
Barky Boats. Calypso has to explain to Bluey and Mackenzie that growing up can be hard.
Go Jetter Heroes: Amelia Earhart. Xuli follows in the footsteps of her hero Amelia Earhart in a transatlantic adventure.
The Tale of Old Brown's New Roost. Animated series. When Old Brown quits Owl Island in search of some quiet, his new roost turns out to be a little too close to home for Peter!
Frigatebird. Tracker and Kwazii help some fish whose food keeps getting stolen by a frigatebird.
Five's Super Sorter. Numberblocks Five and Two are in their toy library, but all their favourite toys are jumbled up. Can their new friend Sorty the Super Sorter help sort out their problem?
Party. The adventures of 26 lively letters who can make words come to life by holding hands. When the Alphablocks' party is a flop, superhero Exciting X comes to the rescue.
Bark. The Nogglins go for a walk in the woods. They discover a maze, and Hum uses her voice to find her way home.
Sharing with Others Day. Love Monster debates whether to share a box of chocolates with his friends or not.
Cats. Bella has fun pretending to be a cat - a Bella-Cat. Together with Lift, she visits Tincy the Spider in the Sing Song Forest, who sings a spider rhyme all about taily-waily animals!
Cranberry. Live from the Roots and Fruits theatre, it's the bouncing and singing Cranberries!
Cake. Children's animation. Bing's friends are coming to his house for a cake party. Bing can't wait to show Sula the cake he's made, but he accidentally tips the cake off its stand.
Chicken. Dee encourages a friend to say 'chicken', and we go on a whirlwind word adventure. We see a white chicken, a pecking chicken, a chilly chicken and even a super chicken!
Margo Mayfly. The bugs help a new bug feel welcome in Shell School.
Piggy Busy Day. The neighbours give the Piggies impossible tasks to cover up a surprise party, but nothing is impossible for the Piggy Builders! Can the neighbours keep the party a secret?
More about High and Low. Stop-motion preschool programme. The Twirlywoos watch people using a lift. Chickedy and Chick follow two people into the lift and go up high with them.
Imaginary Journey. Educational series using Makaton sign language. All aboard for a steam train adventure with Justin and his friends, while Mr Tumble zooms off into space!
Party! Preschool series. It's party time! The Teletubbies have balloons, party blowers and lots of sparkles. In Tummy Tales, twins celebrate their birthday with their friends.
Numbers. Giovanna and Baby Bear invite little ones and their grown-ups to explore numbers through discovery, play, song and story. Babies and grown-ups at home can join in too!
Library. Elsee joins her friend Zakariyya at the library for a game of I-spy.
Birdie. Triggs is finding it hard to get out of bed, and that pesky birdie outside his window isn't helping. Maddie soon helps him discover what the birdie is really singing about.
Doctor. Dylan the doctor faces a puzzling medical case with one of his patients.
Womp Womp Womp. Kiri and Lou are upset with each other but can't remember why.
Bloom Feast. Ginger helps a short Broccoliosaurus reach high flowers with a clever solution.
Mums and Dads. When Indy and Rusty play Mums and Dads, they can't agree on who should go to work.
The Future Badge. The Squirrels are transported into the future, where they encounter a robot version of Duggee as well as a few other interesting changes to the clubhouse.
Watch Out for Tumbleweed! Can the Game Catchers avoid the monster? That's the name of the game on a sandy planet!
Timmy Makes Music. Timmy cannot decide what instrument to play at band practice, so he hangs on to all of them and causes chaos.
Eden and Ava Go Training. Eden and Ava do a workout with Dad and go dog training with cute puppy Sky!
Marian's Bulgarian Gyuveche. Marian's gyuveche is made using layers of peppers, tomatoes and feta with an egg on top.

programmes.show_name: Ferne and Rory's Teeny Tiny Creatures
programmes.show_description: Snails. Rory and Sandy search for snails in the garden and have a snail race. Ferne holds a giant African land snail. Also, find out how snails are part of a large food web.
Mission: Rambler Badge. Dan and Jamie head for the hills to earn their compass badge searching for some missing ramblers and their teachers.
Pygmy Hippo and Aardvark. Aneeshwar and Lizzie meet two amazing animals and reveal a surprising connection between them in the wild. This time, it's a pygmy hippo and some aardvarks.
Waffle the Grand-Dog. Gram and Grand visit. Waffle loves Gram; he'll only leave his new dog doormat for her.
A Feathered Bed. Oona and Baba search for soft bedding for their burrow.
I'm a Little Raindrop. Animated series about a group of singing potatoes. Ruby sings an upbeat techno song, where she imagines what it's like to be a raindrop.
Like Clockwork. When Yukee's precious clockwork music box breaks, her garden friends cheer her up with a life-size clockwork performance of their own.
The Spooky Badge. Animated preschool series. The Squirrels are playing dress-up when they hear a ghostly sound and spot a floating sheet. What is going on?
Bike. Preschool series about words. Dee encourages a friend to say 'bike', and we go on a whirlwind word adventure, seeing lots of different bikes doing lots of different things.
The Pattern of Patterns. In the Pattern Palace, Six sings a song telling us about the different types of pattern all around us. Can you spot them all?
Train. Educational fun. When the Alphablocks go on holiday, a trip on a train is the only way to travel - if only the train would travel somewhere!
Fairies. Educational series using Makaton sign language. Do you believe in fairies? Join Justin and his friends as they visit a fairy forest for a sparkly adventure.
Catch the Ball. Preschool series. The Teletubbies play with a bubble, and then Po and Laa-Laa play catch with a ball. In Tummy Tales, two brothers learn to catch a ball.
Patchwork Hedgehog. The Patchwork Pals have a problem: Hedgehog needs a hug but is way too poky! Will they find a way to help him?
Raa Raa and the Noisy Mystery. Animation set in the Jingly Jangly Jungle. Raa Raa and his friends hear a strange noise and find tracks in the jungle, but what could be making them?
Wilson's Wag-a-Lagon. Will Wilson roll with his long-trusted wagon until the wheels fall off, or can his chuggtastic friends convince him to retire 'wag-a-lagon' before it's too late?
Episode 4. Comedy sketch show for younger viewers. The Berrito Brothers have fallen on even harder times and show off their basketball skills to a small audience.
Grace's Favourite Machines. In this episode, CBeebies' very own motorbike rider, Grace, looks back at some of her favourite machines that are electric, fast and useful.
The Sign Jungle. The Game Catchers land on a new planet with roads and obstacles to play tunnel.
Gulper Eels. Animated deep sea adventures with Captain Barnacles and his explorers. Hungry gulper eels and undersea tremors threaten Inkling and Kwazii.
The Tale of the Great Breakout. Animated series. When Mr McGregor captures the whole squirrel tribe in his garden, Peter must stage a mass breakout to save his friends.
The Space Badge. Animated preschool series. The Squirrels wonder what Duggee is up to, he seems to be looking through a long stick out of the window. What's that, Duggee?
Daphne's Dental Day. Daphne is very excited to show off her clean teeth at the clinic's Dental Day.
The Prickly Pear Kid. Evil Pea sends a load of Mini Peas to infiltrate Tato Tower. There's only one person who can help him - the expert pea-yodeller, Prickly Pear.
Mission Swimming Pool. The ducklings blame Weasy for destroying their swimming pool when they wanted to cool off.
Wingsuit Yourself. Tao needs help learning to fly his new wingsuit for a big town event, and Mojo and Bo are the experts. Can they help him find his inner bird before it's too late?
Pavlova. Mum won't let Bingo have pavlova, so the kids find a way by playing a cheeky game of cafe.
Swim School. Pleasing Big Fish class instructor Margaret is proving difficult for Mum, Dad and Bingo.
The Mysterious Monster. The shadow of a spiky monster causes panic for the Piris, but is all exactly as it seems?
Andy and the Mountain Goats. Preschool wildlife series. Andy heads to the Rocky Mountains of North America in search of a salty rock for the mountain goat enclosure.
Waffle So Far. While home alone with Waffle, Simon remembers all the chaotic times they've shared.
The Cat Wish. Rafi's wish reveals being a cat is hard, and to value Deborah's challenges.
It's Time to Build a Scarecrow. JoJo and Gran Gran make a scarecrow for the community garden.
Hat Fuss. Animation for preschool children. Scarf Lady needs some fresh inspiration and Sarah decides that the hat museum is the perfect place to visit.
Granny and Small. Stop-motion animation. Small is bored, Tiny, Major and Mother are all too busy. Mother suggests he asks Granny to play with him.
Gentle. With MeMo's help, BaBa learns how to be gentle with animals and delicate objects.
Everybody All Aboard the Ninky Nonk. Everybody goes for a ride in the Ninky Nonk but Makka Pakka gets left behind. He chases the Ninky Nonk as he wants to give the Ninky Nonk a wash.
Gaz Coombes - Ella May Does It Her Way. Join Gaz Coombes for a Bedtime Story about a little girl who likes to do things her way but decides to try something new.
This Is CBeebies. Programmes start at 6.00am.
The Tish Tash Detective Agency. Tobby and Jeffy have come to play at Tish's house. It's all fun and games until Dad's blueberry pie is stolen!
Where's Flop? Children's animation. Flop pops to the shop to get some carrots for lunch. Bing stays behind but it is not long before he starts to miss Flop.
Didgeridoo. Preschool series. Tinky Winky learns to play the didgeridoo. The Teletubbies do a special didgeridoo Tubby Phone Dance. Tiddlytubby Ping loves the didgeridoo too.
Hurry Up. Mo is running late, but Tee is moving at his own speed. What's a mum to do when a child is dragging their heels?
Evil Pea Takes a Break. Evil Pea's spa break is ruined when the Cobs try to trick Supertato while she's gone.
Leaf Pictures. Bing and his friends are making leaf pictures, but the wind whisks Coco's picture off the table! Bing catches it by stamping on her picture, and now Coco is angry.
Pizzas Away. Mojo needs to deliver a pizza to a very hard-to-reach island, but some stubborn seabirds have their eye on the pizza. Will Mojo have to go supersonic to get past them?
Frog Song. It's fun on the counting train until Naughty Monkey starts to drive! Ethel makes a shape, Frog drops beats with a ribbit song, and Enid turns into anime to chase after a mouse.
The Family Photo Badge. The Squirrels learn that friends can be like family, and take a brilliant group photo!
Rain. During a heavy summer storm, Bluey is determined to dam the water in the front yard.
Burger Shop. It's time for Bluey and Bingo to get out of the bath, but Dad gets roped into a game.
Tulip Fields, Netherlands. Flower power! Foz and Xuli race to stop a runaway harvester destroying a tulip field.
The Tale of the Tunnel Rumbler. Animated series. When Peter and his friends mistake Jeremy Fisher for a scary monster, they set about ridding their tunnels of the invader.
Madagascar Tsingy. When Selva encounters an injured creature, Peso journeys to provide medical aid.
Back in the Box! The Numberblocks have fun counting as they tidy up after playing games, but One finds it hard to keep track of everything. The friends use tally marks to fix their problem.
Race. The adventures of 26 lively letters who can make words come to life by holding hands. It's race day - will V vroom vroom to victory?
Sand. The Nogglins spend a day at the seaside. There's a shape surprise for all the Nogglins, but will Nono find his?
Bouncy-Slidey Day. Love Monster has to overcome his fears of the big slide to rescue Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny when she gets scared.
Sticks. Bella and Lift play with a stick that makes the perfect magic wand. They visit Patch, who has a tasty recipe - sticky stick critters!
Coconut. Live from the Roots and Fruits theatre, it's Coconut getting his moment in the limelight.
Not Yours. Children's animation. Bing helps Flop do the shopping, but while Flop is paying, Bing finds a strawberry lolly that he can't resist putting in his pocket.
Glasses. Dee encourages a friend to say 'glasses', and we go on a whirlwind word adventure. We see all sorts of glasses - blue glasses, banana glasses, mummy glasses and some funny glasses!
Grow Lines. Lu upsets Grace when she doesn't follow her instructions.
Just a Little Knock. Cesar loves his vehicle, but Wilf crashes it. At first, Cesar isn't worried, but when the vehicle starts acting up, he struggles to admit it's broken.
More About This Way, That Way. Stop-motion preschool programme. The Twirlywoos find themselves in a restaurant and a waiter is taking the diners' orders.
Basketball. Justin plays basketball with his friend, and Mr Tumble has a new bouncy ball.
Custard Chaos. Preschool series. The Tubby Custard Machine careers crazily out of Home Dome and goes on a messy ride around Teletubbyland.
Shops. Nigel and Baby Bear encourage grown-ups and their babies to explore shopping-themed toys and objects, through discovery, play, song and story time. Everyone at home can join in too!
Museum. Elsee plays I-spy at the museum with her new friend Qasim.
Knock Knock. Maddie and Triggs sing about all the things they love regarding their favourite travelling Uncle George as they wait, and wait, and wait for his latest letter to arrive.
Bin Collector. Dylan the bin collector wants to collect all the rubbish. But when his truck overflows, he has to find somewhere to put it!
Good Advice. When Small is feeling strangely unsatisfied, her grandparents suggest she paints more regularly.
The Great Race. Ginger races the Baby Pea-Rexes around Vegesaur Valley to the water hole.
Hide-and-Seek. It's time for a family game of hide-and-seek.
The Philosophy Badge. The Squirrels think about life's big questions, helping Tino the Artistic Mouse escape a crisis of confidence and gaining their Philosophy Badges in the process.
It's Not Time to Nap. The Game Catchers encounter aliens who teach them a game called what time is it, Mr Wolf?
Beep Beep Timmy. Timmy is distraught to find the hooter on his trike is broken but is delighted when Osbourne replaces it.
Purim. We follow Sophia and her family as they celebrate the Jewish holiday of Purim.
Pippa's Zimbabwean Nyama. Pippa is making nyama, a chicken stew that uses tasty spring greens.

programmes.show_name: Ferne and Rory's Teeny Tiny Creatures
programmes.show_description: Butterflies. Rory and Sandy discover that butterflies are important pollinators, and Ferne feeds them.
Mission: Horse Riding. Tinks helps Charli with her first ever riding lesson at Woofton's Clip-Clop Stables.
Porcupine and Three-Banded Armadillo. Aneeshwar and Lizzie meet a porcupine and a three-banded armadillo.
Waffle and Pepper. Vet Jess and Waffle visit the kennels to check on Waffle's new friend Pepper, who is sad. Waffle helps to find out the reason why - snuggly toy PegStar is broken.
Beach Rescue. Baba helps rescue a baby seagull when he gets trapped in fishing twine.
Potato Train. Animated series about a group of singing potatoes. The Small Potatoes hop on board the Potato Train and chug through town, greeting local townspotatoes along the way.
The Piano. Without her ukulele, Yukee struggles to come up with a tune for the words she wrote for a new song. Can the piano Sonny and Paulie the peacock bring to the Sunhouse be the answer?
The Harvest Badge. Animated preschool series. The birds are tweeting and all the crops are looking ripe and ready to pick. Duggee is getting ready to harvest them.
Duck. Preschool series about words. Dee encourages a friend to say 'duck', and we go on a whirlwind word adventure, seeing lots of different coloured ducks.
Grid Unlocked. The Numberblocks turn themselves around to save the Times Tables' 5 by 5 booster grid!
Beep. Educational fun. The Alphablocks meet a metal box that goes beep, and soon it's walking and talking and wishing it could be like its new friends.
Dentist. Justin and his friend go to the dentist while Mr Tumble plays dentist with the toys.
Loop the Loop. Preschool series. In Tummy Tales, a group of children watch a plane loop the loop in the air, whilst the Teletubbies have fun watching a shooting star loop the loop.
Patchwork Gecko. The Patchwork Pals have a problem: Bear is very sad because Gecko lost his tail. Will they find a way to cheer him up?
Raa Raa and the Coolest Hello. Animation set in the Jingly Jangly Jungle. Raa Raa and his friends come up with lots of different ways to say hello to make one cool greeting.
Sleepyhead Koko. Koko let her excitement get the best of her and stayed up all night!
Episode 5. Comedy sketch show for younger viewers. Arthur Sleep gets into a spot of bother when he builds a tall tower in the newsroom.
Flying Machines. Grace does loop the loops in an aerobatic plane, shows an amazing flying taxi and then heads into the clouds in a hang-glider.
The Paper Airplane Race. The Game Catchers land on the Paper-Jungle Planet to play the paper airplane race.
Siphonophore. Animated deep sea adventures with Captain Barnacles and his explorers. Kwazii and Dashi become entangled in a strange deep sea creature, a siphonophore.
The Tale of the Mystery Plum Thief. Animated series. When Peter tries to get the last plum from Mr McGregor's tree, he finds that a mysterious thief has beaten him to it!
The Colour Badge. Animated preschool series. Duggee is painting in his favourite colouring book and the Squirrels want to have a go too.
Zigzag's Skating Booboo. Zigzag is surprised to find she can't rollerskate as fast as she usually runs.
Keep Your Cool. Evil Pea's plans keep failing because Supertato makes her lose her temper. Mummy and Daddy Pea pay a visit to teach her how to keep her cool.
Mission Robot 2.0. Weasy finds a new robot to play with, but the Ducklings aren't so thrilled.
Swophenge. Preston's standing stones aren't standing. Can Mojo's crane top take care of it?
Rain. During a heavy summer storm, Bluey is determined to dam the water in the front yard.
Cafe. Bluey learns that adults take a little bit longer to make friends.
The Piris' Inky Issue. The Piri friends damage a picture in Millie's cave and must fix it before she finds out.
Andy and the African Penguins. Preschool wildlife series. Andy jets off in his safari-mobile to Africa to meet some African penguin chicks.
Waffle at the Kennels. Waffle is staying at Angela's kennels while the Brooklyn-Bells are on holiday.
The Unicorn Wish. Rafi's unicorn wish brings exciting quests, but she realises even heroes need rest - and the best adventures can be found at home.
It's Time for a Tea Party. JoJo and Gran Gran go to a Tyrannosaurus tea party at the museum!
Dewy Morning. Animation for preschool children. Sarah and Duck are so keen to visit the art shop, they get there too early and have to find things to keep themselves entertained.
Tiny's Good Idea. Stop-motion animation. It is hot in Mother's garden and her flowers are wilting. Major tries to help but it doesn't work. Tiny comes up with a brilliant idea.
Looky Tube. MeMo and BaBa play with a cardboard tube and learn to look at the world together.
The Prettiest Flower. Upsy Daisy wants to look at the pretty flowers in the garden, but Igglepiggle wants to ride in the Ninky Nonk. Then Igglepiggle finds the prettiest flower.
Alice Dearing - Tomorrow I'll Be Brave. Swimmer Alice Dearing reads Tomorrow I'll Be Brave, by Jessica Hische.
